race.name: L'√Йtape Slovenia by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'√Йtape Slovenia by Tour de France
race.tagline: Official Tour de France amateur cycling event in Slovenia's scenic Gorenjska region with pro-level closed-road routes.

climate.overview: Early September in Kranj/Gorenjska typically features mild daytime temperatures with possible rain or fog in the foothills.

terrain.overview: Hilly paved roads through Gorenjska region with climbs in the Julian Alps foothills.

logistics.explanation: Easy access from Ljubljana airport, central Kranj base, but limited field size and registration details.

length.explanation: Solid 80km and 150km options suit varied abilities in a compact region.

technicality.explanation: Paved roads with standard road racing demands, no gravel or tech features.

elevation.explanation: Moderate climbs at 841m-2118m, challenging but not Alpine extreme.

climate.explanation: Mild September weather ideal for cycling, low extreme risk.

adventure.explanation: Scenic Julian Alps routes with pro closed-road experience.

prestige.explanation: Official Tour de France √Йtape series branding adds credibility.

race_quality.explanation: Pro mechanics, traffic-free, ambassador Matej MohoriƒН elevate quality.

experience.explanation: Festival village, official jerseys, Tour ties create immersive event.

community.explanation: Tied to Slovenian cycling heritage, growing local appeal.

field_depth.explanation: Amateur focus with uncertain numbers, no elite depth reported.

value.explanation: Early-bird deals and pro features offer good bang for buck.

expenses.explanation: Standard European event costs, early registration helps.

cultural_impact.explanation: Boosts local Kranj cycling scene, linked to MohoriƒН legacy.

biased_opinion.overview: A gem for road enthusiasts seeking Tour de France vibes in Slovenia's stunning foothills. The 150km Race delivers hilly suffering with pro polish, while Matej MohoriƒН's ambassadorship ties it to top-tier heritageвАФperfect mid-tier adventure without Alpine brutality.

final_verdict.one_liner: Pro-level amateur road event with Slovenian flair and Julian Alps scenery.
final_verdict.full_verdict: L'√Йtape Slovenia stands out in the √Йtape series for its accessible yet challenging routes from Kranj, blending Tour branding, closed roads, and local hero appeal. Ideal for sportive riders chasing prestige without overcrowding.

logistics.overview: Start/finish at Slovenski trg in Kranj; registrations via Prijavim.se with early-bird incentives.
logistics.accommodation: Kranj hotels and Gorenjska options; event village on-site.
logistics.registration_notes: Same fee for both distances; early registration by Nov 25, 2024 for 2025 prizes like signed MohoriƒН items.

history.overview: Founded 2022 as part of official L'√Йtape Series; annual September event hosted by Municipality of Kranj and Visit Kranj. Editions: 2022 (Sep 3-4), 2023, 2024 (Sep 7-8), 2025 (Sep 6-7), 2026 (Sep 5-6 planned).
history.founder: Municipality of Kranj and Visit Kranj, inspired by Tour de France.
notable_moments: 2025 early registration prizes: signed bibs, VIP starts.

course_description.character: Scenic, hilly loops through Gorenjska villages and foothills; traffic-free paved roads with pro support.
course_description.signature_challenge: Race's second loop via Besnica, Jamnik, Kropa, GolnikвАФhilly test in Julian Alps foothills.

guide_variables.registration_cost: Uncertain; early-bird cheapest, opened Nov 11, 2024 for 2025

citations.title: L'√Йtape Slovenia by Tour de France
citations.label: Official Website

research_metadata.unique_features: MohoriƒН ambassador, Gorenjska scenery, two distances + corporate stage

videos.title: Join Tadej Pogacar in Slovenia рЯЗЄрЯЗЃ for l'Etape Slovenia by Tour de France
videos.channel: L'Etape Series by Tour de France
videos.description:
Welcome to Slovenia рЯЗЄрЯЗЃ, the home of champions !
On September 4th, experience the Tour de France Legend in Kranj and discover Tadej Pogacar training routes.

With the promo code "ifeelslovenia" get 20вВђ discount till 24th of July 2022.
More info about the event вЮ°пЄП https://bit.ly/LetapeSlovenia

---------

L'Etape by Tour de France is a series of amateur cycling events designed to experience the Tour de France Legend. It is the only event where you can race and feel like a Tour de France champion, and where you can enjoy the Tour de France unique atmosphere and passion.


вАЬThe Tour de France is the world's greatest cycling race. Each year it is passionately followed by fans around the world so it has naturally given rise to L'Etape by Tour de France seriesвАЭ, says Christian Prudhomme, Tour de France director. 

The series has now expanded to more than 20 international locations including:
рЯЗЂрЯЗЈ France
рЯЗІрЯЗЈ Brasil
рЯЗ≤рЯЗљ M√©xico
рЯЗ®рЯЗі Colombia
рЯЗ®рЯЗ≥ China
рЯЗ¶рЯЗЇ Australia
рЯЗєрЯЗ≠ Thailand

рЯЗЃрЯЗ© Indonesia
рЯЗ™рЯЗЄ Spain
рЯЗ≤рЯЗЊ Malaysia
рЯЗЄрЯЗ∞ Slovakia
рЯЗ®рЯЗњ Czech Republic
рЯЗђрЯЗІ UK
рЯЗ®рЯЗ¶ Canada
рЯЗЇрЯЗЄ USA
рЯЗ≥рЯЗ± Netherlands
рЯЗ®рЯЗЊ Cyprus
рЯЗЄрЯЗЃ Slovenia
рЯЗ™рЯЗ® Ecuador

Among these Tour de France official cycling events is of course L'√Йtape du Tour. With a peloton of 15 000 riders and 50 nationalities, it is Europe's biggest cycling event. Riding on the same routes and under the same conditions as Tour de France champions: this is the promise that L'√Йtape du Tour has kept since 1993.

Check out https://www.letapebytourdefrance.com/ for more information.

videos.title: Primoz Roglic ZERO cycling to PRO in 1 year
videos.description:
Primo≈Њ was born and raised in Trbovlje. 

He is the only child to father, Polde Roglic a former miner, and mother Anita RogliƒН.

At the age of 10, he was already skiing the local jump at Zagorje ob Savi.

вАЬIt was like flying. I liked the feeling. ItвАЩs something everyone dreams of doing,вАЭ Roglich said. вАЬI wanted to be the best ski jumper in the world.вАЭ

At 17-year-old he won a gold medal at the world championships in 2007. A few weeks later, he ventured to Planica, site of one of the most notorious ski jumps in the world. It is here that RogliƒН suffered a huge crash with his shoulder and head taking the brunt of the impact resulting In being airlifted to hospital. 

Even though he broke his nose and suffered a concussion, he persisted with ski jumping for another four years. His team won the silver medal at the junior world cup in ski jumping in Kranj in 2008.  

He finally said goodbye to ski jumping in 2012. вАЬI felt it was time for a change,вАЭ RogliƒН recounted. вАЬI liked cycling and thought, why not? I will try to become professional.вАЭ

вАЬSki jumping is a different sport, but there is a lot of stretching, yoga, speed work and core training,вАЭ RogliƒН said. вАЬThe skills are different, but I think that those skills helped me as a cyclist.вАЭ

вАЬWhen I was a ski jumper, I was not allowed to ride the bike because it would bulk you up,вАЭ RogliƒН said. вАЬWe did a lot of work with core strength, balance, flexibility and acrobatics. All that helps me on the bike.вАЭ

Hauptman, now a sport director at¬†UAE-Emirates¬†as well as the national coach for the Slovenian Olympic team, remembers it well.

He called me one dayвАФI had no idea who he wasвАФand he said, вАШhello, I am Primo≈Њ and I want to become a professional bike racer,вАЩвАЭ Hauptman said with a laugh. вАЬI said, how old are you, 21? ItвАЩs not so easy, cycling, especially starting from zero. He told me he used to be a sportsman, and I thought, OK, if he was a runner or a cross-country skier, but when he said ski jumper, I just laughed. I told him, OK, get a bike and call me back. I never thought I would hear from him again.вАЭ

He sold his motorcycle and worked at a supermarket to save up money to buy a second-hand racing bike, and called Hauptman back. As Hauptman recalls, RogliƒН crashed more than he won.
вАЬHis big problem was staying in the group without crashing,вАЭ he said. вАЬOnce he was alone or on the climbs, you could see he had something special. He had a huge engine.вАЭ

The first hints of RogliƒНвАЩs raw power came during training rides. One day, the team rode five hours at race speed and ended with a climb, and the only rider who could beat him was Jan Polanc.

вАЬHe made mistakes, but only once. He was mature, focused and very strong mentally. He believed in himself,вАЭ Hauptman said. вАЬHe really started at zero, and now he is a hero. ItвАЩs incredible.вАЭ

By 2013, just over a year after starting cycling, he had his first pro contract. He joined Adria Mobil, a Slovenian continental team. His first pro win came in his second season, taking a stage at the Tour dвАЩAzerba√ѓdjan.

At 22, Roglich went in for testing at a top Slovenian sports lab. According to the German newspaper Deutsche Welle, Roglich revealed a V02max of 80.2, close to the top numbers posted by Chris Froome or Egan Bernal at the time.

In 2015 Jumbo-Visma sport director Frans Maasen recalls how the team was made aware of Primoz. вАЬI got a call from a Slovenian friend, he said, вАШI have quite a story for you. I have found this ski jumper who has a huge motor and can ride like no one else.вАЩ He wouldnвАЩt stop talking about him,вАЭ вАЬAt first, I didnвАЩt think much of it. We flew him to Netherlands to take a test. He was like a Ferrari.вАЭ

RogliƒНвАЩs core strength and flexibility help him with his time trial position, so much so that he almost beat Dumoulin in his first grand tour time-trial stage.

joined Jumbo-Visma in 2016.

By 2016, just four years after he started to train and race, RogliƒН was at the WorldTour. Impressed by his capacity, the team brought him to the 2016 Jiro, his first grand tour. On his first day of his first grand tour, he nearly beat Doomoulin. Nine days later, he won a rolling, 40.5km individual time trial.

I have never seen a rider learn so fast what it takes to race at this level,вАЭ said Jumbo-Visma sports director Addy Engels. вАЬYou have to remember that he did not start racing until he was 21. And now he has won a grand tour in only his fifth one. That is impressive.вАЭ

вАЬHeвАЩs pretty hard to read,вАЭ Bennett said. вАЬHe keeps his cards pretty close to his chest. But what he can do on the bike is impressive. He is almost freakish in how strong he is.вАЭ

Teammates describe RogliƒН, by use words like вАЬfocused,вАЭ вАЬdriven,вАЭ and someone who is вАЬdetailed-oriented.вАЭ

вАЬHeвАЩs a great leader to work for. He stays calm in every situation, and that carries over to us,вАЭ said teammate Sepp Kuss. вАЬThings donвАЩt faze him. He brushes things off. He says things that are uniquely Primo≈Њ.вАЭ

rider_intel.search_text: L'√Йtape Slovenia by Tour de France is a 75-mile gravel event with 5900 feet of elevation in Kranj, Slovenia. The race route passes through roads that helped shape professional cyclist Tadej PogaƒНar's development, as he mentions riders will cycle through his hometown of Kranj and the surrounding area. The event is positioned as offering beautiful roads through the Slovenian countryside that were formative for one of cycling's biggest stars. Limited specific course details are available from rider accounts, but the event promises scenic terrain through the region that produced world-class cycling talent.
